title: commelina calandrinioides
intro: Commelina calandrinioides, also known as the wandering Jew or creeping Jacob, is a species of flowering plant in the dayflower family (Commelinaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Commelina calandrinioides include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
